files.content:
# Phytron MCC-1 MINI-W-ETH database scaffold
#
# PV set required by task:
# - POS_RBV
# - MOVE_PLUS
# - MOVE_MINUS
# - STOP
# - SPEED_RBV
#
# Notes:
# - This database uses StreamDevice protocol separation via phytron.proto.
# - Address handling is currently hardcoded in phytron.proto using the observed working
#   address '0'. If later commissioning requires configurable address support, propagate
#   ADDR from st.cmd -> dbLoadRecords() -> protocol payload construction.
# - MOVE_PLUS / MOVE_MINUS / STOP are command records using bo with HIGH=0 so the write
#   occurs immediately when set to 1 from a client.

record(ai, "$(P)$(R)POS_RBV") {
    field(DESC, "Phytron position readback")
    field(DTYP, "stream")
    field(INP,  "@phytron.proto getPosition $(PORT)")
    field(SCAN, "1 second")
    field(PREC, "0")
    field(EGU,  "steps")
}

record(ai, "$(P)$(R)SPEED_RBV") {
    field(DESC, "Phytron speed readback")
    field(DTYP, "stream")
    field(INP,  "@phytron.proto getSpeed $(PORT)")
    field(SCAN, "2 second")
    field(PREC, "0")
    field(EGU,  "steps/s")
}

record(bo, "$(P)$(R)MOVE_PLUS") {
    field(DESC, "Phytron jog/move plus")
    field(DTYP, "stream")
    field(OUT,  "@phytron.proto movePlus $(PORT)")
    field(ZNAM, "Idle")
    field(ONAM, "Move+")
    field(HIGH, "0")
}

record(bo, "$(P)$(R)MOVE_MINUS") {
    field(DESC, "Phytron jog/move minus")
    field(DTYP, "stream")
    field(OUT,  "@phytron.proto moveMinus $(PORT)")
    field(ZNAM, "Idle")
    field(ONAM, "Move-")
    field(HIGH, "0")
}

record(bo, "$(P)$(R)STOP") {
    field(DESC, "Phytron stop motion")
    field(DTYP, "stream")
    field(OUT,  "@phytron.proto stopMotion $(PORT)")
    field(ZNAM, "Idle")
    field(ONAM, "Stop")
    field(HIGH, "0")
}

#!../../bin/linux-x86_64/phytronIOC
# Startup script for Phytron MCC-1 MINI-W-ETH IOC scaffold
#
# Debug / commissioning notes:
# - TCP port is verified: 22222
# - Framed protocol style is verified: STX=0x02, ETX=0x03
# - Observed working controller address was '0'; previous failed setup used '1'
# - Current phytron.proto hardcodes address '0' in the payload bytes.
#   If hardware does not respond, first verify network reachability, then confirm
#   the MCC-1 address and adjust phytron.proto payload address bytes as needed.
# - asyn trace is enabled below to simplify bring-up and packet verification.

< envPaths

cd "${TOP}"

## Register database definitions
dbLoadDatabase "dbd/phytronIOC.dbd"
phytronIOC_registerRecordDeviceDriver pdbbase

## User-configurable macros for deployment
epicsEnvSet("P", "PHYTRON:")
epicsEnvSet("R", "M1:")
epicsEnvSet("PORT", "MCC1")
epicsEnvSet("HOST", "192.168.0.10")
epicsEnvSet("TCP_PORT", "22222")
# Address macro retained here as documentation for future parameterization.
# Current protocol file still hardcodes address '0'.
epicsEnvSet("ADDR", "0")

## Configure asyn IP port for TCP/IP connection
## noAutoConnect=0 so the IOC attempts connection during startup
drvAsynIPPortConfigure("$(PORT)", "$(HOST):$(TCP_PORT)", 0, 0, 0)

## Enable useful asyn tracing during bring-up
asynSetTraceMask("$(PORT)", 0, 0x9)
asynSetTraceIOMask("$(PORT)", 0, 0x2)

## Load records
dbLoadRecords("db/phytron.db", "P=$(P),R=$(R),PORT=$(PORT)")

cd "${TOP}/iocBoot/${IOC}"
iocInit

## Optional: increase trace later from the IOC shell if deeper debugging is needed.
## Key commissioning checks:
## 1) Confirm TCP connect to $(HOST):$(TCP_PORT)
## 2) Confirm framed bytes on wire begin with 0x02 and end with 0x03
## 3) Confirm address byte in phytron.proto matches controller configuration
## 4) Replace placeholder command payloads after capture verification
